A COMPLAINT "NEED ONLY ALLEGE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION; EACH EVIDENTIARY FACT THAT MIGHT EVENTUALLY FORM PART OF THE PLAINTIFF'S PROOF NEED NOT BE ALLEGED." (C.A. V. WILLIAM S. HART UNION HIGH SCHOOL DIST. (2012) 53 CAL.4TH 861, 872.) THE CROSS-COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES FACTS TO STATE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR CONTRACT BREACH AND CONVERSION; EVERY "FOUNDATIONAL FACT" NEED NOT BE PLED. (ID.) ORAL REPRESENTATIONS AND A PRIOR ONGOING BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP ARE EXPLICITLY ALLEGED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER , CLERK: W. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2019 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS C.H.A CORP, GIDEON CHAMDI's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendants' demurrers to the second amended complaint are overruled in their entirety.
Causes of action 1 (breach of written contract), 3 (specific performance), 7 (Bus. & Prof. Code 17200) and 8 (fraud) do not differ between the first and second amended complaints and demurrers to them were already overruled on September 26, 2018. No reconsideration motion was filed. (Benefit v. Suncloud 1997) 56 Cal.App.4th 91, 96-97.)

As to other causes of action:
2 (breach of oral agreement), 4 (account stated) and 5 (open book) are all appropriately pled in the alternative. 6 (voidable transaction): adequately pled. 10 (breach of Corporations Code 1903, 2004): private rights of action exist, and breach is adequately pled. 11 (violation of Civil Code 3439.04): alter ego adequately pled. 12: (Bus. & Prof. Code 17200): adequately pled.

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANTS BAY AREA DIAMOND WHOLESALERS, INC. and AMNON OSHRI'S DEMURRERS TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT

OVERRULED. A complaint "need only allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action; each evidentiary fact that might eventually form part of the plaintiff's proof need not be alleged." (C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 872.) The cross-complaint sufficiently alleges facts to state causes of action for contract breach and conversion; every "foundational fact" need not be pled. (Id.) Oral representations and a prior ongoing business relationship are explicitly alleged.

Order signed

MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT C.H.A CORP, GIDEON CHAMDI'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants demur to five causes of action in plaintiff's first amended complaint (FAC). The demurrers are OVERRULED in their entirety:

1st (Breach of Written Contract): The FAC pleads that its exhibit B combined with the parties' custom and practice to form a contract; no conflict appears on the FAC's face.

3rd (Specific Performance): Though technically a remedy, specific performance is often pled as a "cause of action;" Rogers v. Davis (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 1215 did not find that practice demurrable. Specific performance and damages may be pled in the alternative. (Id. at 1220.)

7th (Business & Professions Code 17200): At a minimum, fraudulent business practices are pled.

8th (Fraud): At a minimum, deceit and false promise fraud are adequately pled.

9th (Conversion): Conversion is adequately pled.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
